mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with robust monthly payroll growth indicate sustained consumer spending power, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts short-term equity momentum over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Negative correlation persists across all quarterly observations from 2020 to 2023, or forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales forecasts signals weakening consumer spending, which reduces near-term earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 12-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth confirms broad economic expansion, sustaining corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Negative forward returns occur in > 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below -0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates exceed 4%, simultaneous labor market cooling amplifies recession fears, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate when both conditions hold, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation combined with overheating payrolls forces prolonged monetary restraint, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns exceed 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
